Now it's time to take our final step, which is to create a plan. We've all been in long meetings that ended with no action plan. We want to avoid that whenever possible. So after everyone presents their ideas, take 10 to 15 minutes as a group to select and prioritize the best ideas. But here's an important piece of advice. Creating a plan doesn't mean creating a script to follow. Scripts are not ideal because circumstances in the real world are dynamic and they often don't let you stick to a script. The plan you should create can be just a set of bullet points of your best ideas in the order you are going to implement them. In this way, you can create your action plan during the meeting instead of afterward, which allows everyone to leave with an action plan in mind. Not only is this an efficient way to attack the current influence challenge, it will likely raise the motivation of your colleagues to come to the next session as well. By the way, you can download the PDF guidelines for the influence process in this module. So you can bring it to every meeting. I wish you good luck generating your best ideas in the most effective, efficient and ethical way.
